hints.An: 'An' is a conjunction introducing a question — 'or' / 'is it that'
hints.invidiam: 'invidiam' is a noun in the accusative case
hints.posteritatis: 'posteritatis' is a noun in the genitive case

hints.to time: Latin has no verb 'to time' — 'timere' means 'to fear/be afraid of'

reveal.gloss: you fear / are you afraid of

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'timeas' — 'times' is indicative

hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the parts of 'tenere' (to hold) — a different 2nd conjugation verb
hints.tango, tangere, tetigi, tactum: These are the parts of 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.tollo, tollere, sustuli, sublatum: These are the parts of 'tollere' (to lift/remove)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'invidia' — 'invidiam' with -am ending is accusative, object of 'times'

hints.2nd: 2nd declension accusative ends in -um — 'invidia, -ae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusative ends in -em — 'invidia, -ae' is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension accusative ends in -em — 'invidia, -ae' is 1st declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'posteritas' — 'posteritatis' with -atis ending is genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive ends in -ae — 'posteritas, posteritatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive ends in -i — 'posteritas, posteritatis' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension genitive ends in -ei — 'posteritas, posteritatis' is 3rd declension

steps.prompt: What does 'An' signal at the start of a question?
steps.target: The second part of a disjunctive question — 'Or is it that …?'
choices: The second part of a disjunctive question — 'Or is it that …?'

hints.A simple yes/no question: Simple yes/no questions use '-ne' or 'num' — 'an' introduces an alternative in a double question
hints.A negative question: Negative questions use 'nonne' — 'an' offers an alternative possibility
hints.A rhetorical statement: 'An' does introduce a rhetorical question, but specifically as the second alternative in a disjunctive question

translation: Or do you fear the resentment of posterity?
